title: G Parameshwara set to be Karnataka deputy CM as Congress finalises DKS cabinet
content: G Parameshwara is set to become Karnataka's Deputy Chief Minister as Congress finalizes its cabinet. The decision, made after high-level meetings, aims to balance caste and regional interests. Chief Minister-designate D K Shivakumar's swearing-in is scheduled for June 3rd, with a significant number of ministers expected to take the oath alongside him.

title: RCB batter who left IPL midway is 'absolutely fine' to play in ENG vs NZ Test
content: Bethell had cut short his stint with IPL 2026 champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru so he could return home and be assessed by England's medical team ahead of the New Zealand series.

title: Microsoft tells engineers to stop using Anthropic's Claude
content: Microsoft is discontinuing most internal Claude Code licenses by June 30, directing engineers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI. This move follows Claude Code's unexpected popularity, which reportedly undercut Microsoft's homegrown product. While officially citing toolchain unification, the timing suggests cost-cutting amidst an industry-wide AI spending crunch.

title: Google plans to release 32mn Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida, California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su: Companies do not need people who know how to use AI tools
content: AMD CEO Lisa Su advised graduates entering the AI-driven job market to prioritize purpose, judgment, and problem-solving over simply learning AI tools. She emphasized that employers need individuals who can determine AI's application, not just operate the technology. Su highlighted that human judgment remains crucial for deciding which problems are worth solving and taking responsibility for outcomes.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Colts, Bills emerge as landing spots for Steelers QB
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers’ popular quarterback Mason Rudolph is looking to be traded in the new season with Aaron Rodgers gearing up for his last year on the field as many wait for him to bounce back on the field in the new season. Amid all this, the Indianapolis Colts and the Buffalo Bills have been linked to Mason Rudolph for the upcoming season.

title: Josh Jacobs’ Packers future takes new turn amid Ash Kaashh scrutiny
content: The Green Bay Packers’ star player Josh Jacobs has remained distant from the controversial legal situation he has found himself in, sparking a heated debate among fans. While the star player was released from police custody last week, rumors swirl about Josh Jacobs’ uncertain future with the Green Bay Packers in the new season.

title: Maple Leafs face coaching setback after David Carle rejects proposal
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs are facing new hurdles in their search for a head coach after David Carle chose not to pursue the position. Now general manager John Chayka needs to look at other candidates and answer questions about the team’s future. The move comes in a pivotal offseason as Toronto looks to get back to competing in the NHL.

title: Data vs. dahi-chini: Why AI can code your life, but only your mom can decode your face
content: AI offers structured advice for life's challenges, processing vast data to simulate reasoning. However, a mother's instinct, honed over decades, provides a deeper, more personal form of comfort and guidance. This contrast highlights the irreplaceable nature of maternal love and understanding in navigating everyday crises.

title: 'Unexpected' but accepted: Cricket Canada breaks silence on ICC suspension
content: The ICC suspended Cricket Canada with immediate effect following its Board meeting in Ahmedabad on Sunday, citing what it called "serious breaches" of membership obligations. At the same time, the ICC clarified that Canadian national teams would remain eligible to participate in ICC events so that players are not affected by the governance issues.

title: 'No role for third parties': India slams Nepal after PM Shah's UK remark on border row
content: India has firmly rejected any third-party involvement in resolving its boundary dispute with Nepal, asserting that bilateral mechanisms are in place. This response follows Nepal's Prime Minister's suggestion to include China and the UK in discussions. While most of the border is demarcated, unresolved segments and land usage issues persist.

title: Dehradun woman locked in toilet for 10 months, assaulted by in-laws after twins' birth
content: A shocking case of domestic violence has emerged from Dehradun. A 32-year-old woman endured 10 months of confinement in a toilet. Her in-laws allegedly subjected her to brutal assaults, including attacks on her private parts. She was also separated from her newborn twins shortly after birth. The woman was reportedly fed only plain rice and onions during her ordeal.

title: Arjun Erigaisi becomes only Indian chess player in FIDE top 10 after latest rankings update
content: Indian Grandmaster Arjun Erigaisi has returned to the world's top 10, climbing to No. 8 in the June 2026 FIDE ratings after gaining 10 points. He is the only Indian in the top 10. Strong performances, including at the TePe Sigeman tournament in Malmö, boosted his ranking. Although he lost a playoff to Magnus Carlsen, Erigaisi's overall results secured his comeback among chess's elite.

title: How did Taiwan, Seoul overtake India? Drop from 5th to 7th largest stock market - explained
content: At a time of global economic turmoil, why are some global markets like the US, Taiwan and South Korea rallying? Why has the Indian stock market slipped from record highs, giving negative returns over the last few quarters?

title: Kangana Ranaut backs Ranveer Singh amid FWICE ban over ‘Don 3’ row
content: Ranveer Singh faces a ban from FWICE and IMPPA following a dispute with producer-director Farhan Akhtar over 'Don 3'. Kangana Ranaut has voiced support for Singh, drawing parallels to her own experiences with industry bans and asserting that increased status often brings adversaries. She believes Singh will overcome these challenges, comparing it to her own successful career despite obstacles.

title: Unmanned T‑72 tanks: Army's workhorse gets digital makeover for future wars
content: The Indian Army is modernizing its T-72 tanks by planning to convert them into remotely operated vehicles, integrating them into a manned-unmanned teaming framework. This initiative aims to enhance combat power and survivability by deploying these digitized tanks for high-risk missions, feeding real-time data to command networks.  This cost-effective approach repurposes legacy armour, aligning with India's indigenous defense innovation push.

title: ‘Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata’ trailer: Kangana Ranaut shines in 26/11 thriller
content: Kangana Ranaut plays a brave nurse in 'Bharat Bhagya Vidhata', inspired by the true story of hospital staff at Mumbai's Cama and Albless hospitals who saved nearly 400 patients during the 26/11 terror attacks. Directed by Manoj Taparia, the film releases on June 12th alongside three other major releases.

title: Can a delayed chargesheet get an accused bail? Kerala high court explains in drug case
content: The Kerala High Court made headlines by ruling that late e-filing of police reports—specifically after the 5 pm cutoff—gives accused parties a right to statutory bail. Justice Kauser Edappagath set free two individuals detained for drug possession, reiterating that the enforcement of filing deadlines is vital to maintaining fairness and protecting the rights of those in the legal system.

title: How Sooryavanshi combined Kohli & Gayle to produce greatest IPL season
content: If the question is which batter produced the most extreme combination of volume, speed and power across a single IPL season, the evidence points overwhelmingly in one direction. The IPL has seen bigger run tallies. It has seen cleaner six-hitters. What it had never seen before 2026 was a player score nearly 800 runs while striking at 237 and smashing 72 sixes. For 18 seasons, volume and violence existed separately. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi merged them, and that is why his IPL campaign 2026 may be one of the greatest batting seasons ever.

title: 30 years of Suryakiran: How India's red arrows became a symbol of precision and national aspiration
content: For three decades, the Suryakiran Aerobatic Team has showcased the Indian Air Force's precision and technological evolution. Completing 30 years, the team's journey mirrors the IAF's growth, inspiring billions and strengthening diplomatic ties globally. They stand among the world's elite nine-aircraft aerobatic teams, a testament to their unwavering pursuit of excellence.

title: The ‘giant leap’ for mankind: Inside NASA’s bold plan to build a Lunar City
content: NASA is launching an innovative mission to create a lasting presence on the Moon with a new base located near the lunar south pole. This exciting venture will unfold in multiple stages, starting with robotic explorers setting up the foundation, followed by human habitation.

title: Annamalai submits resignation letter to BJP chief; to launch new party in 6-8 months
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP president K Annamalai met with party leaders in Delhi, submitting his resignation amid speculation of launching a new regional party.  Associates suggest the outfit will have a "secular" and "Tamil-first" outlook, aiming to be an alternative to existing political forces in the state.

title: '15 lakh hits within 2 mins': CBSE re-evaluation portal faces massive cyberattack
content: CBSE reported that its re-evaluation portal faced multiple cyberattack attempts while processing over 16,000 student submissions for verification and re-evaluation of Class 12 answer sheets. The board said a denial-of-service attack generated nearly 1.5 million hits within two minutes, along with over one lakh unauthorized access attempts. Despite the disruptions, the platform remained operational. The portal, delayed for infrastructure upgrades, will stay open until June 6 with extended session limits and improved stability for applicants.

title: Strong El Niño likely this year, climate change could intensify extreme weather: WMO
content: The World Meteorological Organization warns of an 80% chance of a strong El Niño developing this year, potentially amplifying extreme weather events due to climate change. This phenomenon, characterized by warmer Pacific waters, could lead to intensified heatwaves, droughts, and floods globally. Scientists fear this combination may make 2027 the hottest year on record.

title: NEET MDS 2026 result out: Direct link to download scorecards
content: The National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS) has released the NEET MDS 2026 results today, June 2. Candidates can now access their scorecards and the merit list on the official website, natboard.edu.in. Over 30,000 candidates appeared for the examination, and qualified individuals can now proceed to the counselling process for MDS admissions.

title: Thinking of filing for divorce? Here are the documents you should collect first
content: Navigating divorce can be overwhelming, but thorough documentation is essential for pivotal decisions regarding alimony, child custody, and property distribution. Be prepared by collecting marriage records, identification, financial statements, property titles, and any relevant evidence. For custody disputes, focus on child-related documents. Getting organized in advance paves the way for a less stressful legal journey.

title: Extreme heat tests India's record milk production
content: Extreme heat means cows ‌eat less, make less milk, struggle to conceive and produce fewer live offspring, experts say, while farmers spend more trying to keep animals cool and fertile

title: Sarthak Sidhant flags CBSE OSM tender irregularities before Parliamentary panel
content: 17-year-old from Jharkhand presents findings before Standing Committee on Education as CBSE defends process, promises fixes

title: Venezuela’s Acting President Delcy Rodriguez to visit India
content: Ms. Rodriguez will pay a visit to India from June 3 to 7 and she will be accompanied by Ministers of Foreign Affairs, Economy and Finance, Science and Technology, Communication and Information and Transportation

title: CBSE transfers are a cover-up; govt. must sack Dharmendra Pradhan: Congress
content: “A one-member ‘inquiry’ committee - Formed. And the real culprit, Dharmendra Pradhan - Safe. Officials removed. Minister spared. This isn’t accountability — it’s a cover-up,” Rahul Gandhi posted on X

title: CBSE Chairman, Secretary replaced amid OSM row
content: Centre appoints Lokhande Prashant Sitaram as new CBSE chair, replacing Rahul Singh, who has been pushed out to the Agri Ministry; sets up one-member committee to probe procurement of services for CBSE’s OSM portal

title: Display fee structure at entrance by June 5, private schools told
content: The Directorate of Private Schools has issued the directive after the Tamil Nadu Information Commission ruled recently that the school fee structure be displayed at the entrance of the schools

title: Chief Minister Vijay failing to uphold constitutional morality, says A. Raja
content: The DMK could wait six months before offering a broader assessment of Vijay’s government, but that did not mean it would refrain from commenting on day-to-day governance, says party deputy general secretary

title: SCTIMST successfully performs first LDL apheresis procedure for treating Familial Hypercholesterolemia
content: Procedure performed on a 34-year-old woman suffering from Homozygous Familial Hypercholesterolemia, a rare genetic condition, which results in extremely high cholesterol levels from a young age

title: CBSE portal for re-evaluation faces glitches on first day; Aadhaar authentication made mandatory
content: The CBSE opened the portal on June 2, to enable applications for verification of issues observed in the scanned copies of answer sheets it had provided, and re-evaluation of answers; the portal would remain open till the midnight of June 6, the Board said.

title: Indian students top UK higher educational applications, says official
content: Inaugurating the Tirupati branch of Academic Overseas Private Limited, UK Deputy High Commissioner (Andhra Pradesh and Telangana) says several of the prestigious universities in the UK continue to rank among the top institutions in the world

title: No place for third parties in border issues: MEA in response to Nepal PM while hosting country’s ruling party chief
content: MEA spokesperson responds to claims made by Balen Shah who had asserted that the boundary issue is a complex one with both India and Nepal having possession of each other’s territory and had sought intervention of the U.K. and China

title: Kerala launches ‘Operation Toofan’ to combat growing drug menace
content: Chief Minister cautions that State’s hard-earned social progress and its youth are under direct threat from an organised drug mafia

title: SCR continues in key role as South Coastal Railway takes off as 18th zone
content: The SCR’s network length now stands at 3,603 km - down from 6,645 km earlier - comprising Secunderabad (1,835 km), Hyderabad (782 km), and Nanded (986 km) divisions

title: Will continue to fight BJP till they are defeated, says Mamata
content: Amidst talk of defection from the Trinamool Congress, Mamata Banerjee challenged her detractors who sought to create a ‘new Trinamool’

title: Congress targets PM Modi over silence on Israel’s Lebanon offensive
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh asks, “Does the so-called fatherland mean much more to him than his actual motherland?”

title: Rahul Gandhi lauds unity among Rajasthan Congress chief and CLP leader
content: Such unity can be model for other States, he says as the two leaders greet him at the airport and jointly offer a bouquet

title: Second and third year convocation held at Nrupathunga Cluster University
content: Karnataka Film Chamber of Commerce president Jayamala Ramachandra, senior litterateur S.G. Siddaramaiah and others, were awarded honorary doctorates on the occasion of convocation ceremony of Nrupathunga Cluster University on Tuesday

title: INDIA bloc to meet on June 8 amid strain
content: 17 Opposition parties likely to join the meeting; all eyes on DMK, given the recent tensions with Congress for switching sides in Tamil Nadu

title: Congress terms Pawan Kalyan’s remark as disrespecting Telangana’s identity
content: Congress leaders accuse him of disrespecting Telangana’s identity and demanded that he apologise before attempting political expansion in the State.

title: Karnataka launches antimicrobial resistance surveillance programme in collaboration with TIGS
content: It is aimed at identifying resistance trends, emerging microbial threats and geographical variations in antimicrobial resistance

title: Pawan Kalyan announces Jana Sena will contest elections in Telangana
content: Pawan Kalyan targeted the Congress Party while praising Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) founder K. Chandrashekhar Rao, notably avoiding any reference to the BJP

title: Patient’s death after falling off cot in Thiruvananthapuram MCH triggers row
content: Wilfred, 62, of Peryam, Kollam, had been admitted at the hospital with severe jaundice and associated neurological issues such as altered sensorium

title: M.V. Govindan demands Kerala government to waive fuel tax revenue amid rising fuel price
content: He accuses Chief Minister of playing opportunistic politics as he had as the Leader of the Opposition taken the position that the government should forgo fuel tax revenue

title: Demography panel may study SIR exclusions; seeks details from government
content: Panel to recommend “well-organised and permanent operational system for the legal, fair and time bound identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants residing in the country”

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Bhopal court sends Samarth and Giribala Singh to 14-day judicial custody
content: Mother-in-law Giribala Singh alleges ‘media trial’, danger to life; retired district judge presents her case herself in court, alleges procedural irregularities on part of the CBI

title: Farmers who gave land for Parandur airport face uncertainty
content: Many who parted with their land for Parandur airport are worried, as they don’t know whether the project would take off. They also demand assured jobs and more financial compensation

title: Use early weather warnings to minimise monsoon losses: IMD chief
content: District administrations, disaster management agencies and line departments urged to use advance forecasts and coordinated preparedness measures to reduce loss of life and property during the Southwest Monsoon, says IMD Director General Mrutyunjay Mohapatra

title: Watch: Iran-U.S. deal uncertain | Mufti calls for Ladakh-like J&K unity | Above the Fold | 02.06.2026
content: From stalled Iran ceasefire talks and reports of a Trump-Netanyahu clash to Mehbooba Mufti’s appeal to New Delhi and Myanmar President U Min Aung Hlaing’s India visit - here are our top stories.

title: School safety: AEOs, DEOs to conduct fresh round of inspections from June 3
content: Decision follows the collapse of the roof of a structure that housed district project office of the Samagra Shiksha Kerala on the campus of Central High School, Attakulangara, in Thiruvananthapuram

title: DRDO, IAF successfully flight-test indigenous RudraM-II air-to-surface missile
content: This marks a significant milestone in India’s efforts to strengthen its precision strike capabilities and advance self-reliance in defence technology

title: Social realities belie notion that marriage severs daughter’s ties with parental family, says Supreme Court
content: The top court ruled that marital status cannot be a ground for denying compassionate appointment to a married daughter

title: Tamil Nadu BJP leader K. Annamalai meets Amit Shah
content: Several media reports claimed that Mr. Annamalai also met BJP president Nitin Nabin and party national general secretary B.L. Santhosh, and tendered his resignation from the party, but there was no official confirmation on this

title: Jagan alleges weakening of Aarogyasri scheme
content: The poor and middle-class families are facing difficulties in accessing quality medical treatment under the NDA dispensation, alleges the YSRCP chief

title: "Dig Of Century": Ancient Coins, Medieval Pottery Unearthed From Notre Dame
content: At the cathedral's birth in 1163, the entire square was packed with medieval houses, split by a single street.

title: Flash Floods, Landslides After 3 Cloudbursts In Jammu And Kashmir
content: The first cloudburst occurred in the Gahan area of Sarthal in Kishtwar, followed by another in Machipal.

title: Delhi Launches Water Rationalisation Plan As Survey Reveals Supply Imbalance
content: A Delhi government survey has found stark differences in water supply across the capital, with some densely populated constituencies receiving far less water per person than areas with much smaller populations.

title: Centre's Big CBSE Move Amid On-Screen Marking Portal Irregularities
content: CBSE Chairman, Secretary Transferred, Panel Formed To Probe Irregularities In On-Screen Marking System

title: Police Summons Ex-Bengal Sports Minister Over Messi Event Mess
content: Aroop Biswas, a TMC leader, was asked to appear before the investigating officers of Bidhannagar Dakshin Police Station on June 5, the police officer said.

title: Punjab Farmer's Daughter Tops Air Force Academy Merit List
content: Both cadets come from modest backgrounds and represent the institute's motto, "Shubh Karman Te Kabhu Na Taraun".

title: 'Brought Shame On Religion': Sikh Man Jailed For Stabbing UK Teen Over Racism
content: Judge William Mousley of the Southampton Crown Court said that Digwa killed Nowak with a large knife that he kept attached to his belt. Mousley said that Nowak asked the Digwa if he was a "bad man", which led him to feel that he was insulted.

title: Won't Scrap 'Ladki Bahin' Scheme, 1.7 Crore Women To Get Benefits: D Fadnavis
content: He noted that the scrutiny revealed anomalies, such as women from families of government employees availing benefits and, in some instances, men filing applications.

title: G Parameshwara, Influential Dalit Leader, To Be DK Shivakumar's Deputy: Sources
content: G Parameshwara wants to be the only deputy to DK Shivakumar, though others are still trying to push for more than one deputy chief minister, sources said

title: 7 Killed, 15 Injured As 2 Maharashtra Buses Collide Head-On In Surat
content: Surat, June 2 (IANS) At least seven people were killed and around 15 others injured after a major road accident involving two Maharashtra State Transport (ST) buses and a tractor near Uva-Manekpor village in Bardoli taluka of Gujarat's Surat district

title: Urban Indian Women's Obesity at 42.8%, Nearly Double Rural Rate
content: The National Family Health Survey (NFHS-6) 2023-24 classifies obesity and overweight if the Body Mass Index (BMI) is 25 kg per square metre or above, among people aged 15-49 years.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Delhi Records Coolest Start To June In 3 Years
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Delhi experiences lowest June temperatures in three years and moderate air quality index of 105.

title: Climate Change Will Make Super El Nino Especially Severe, Scientists Warn
content: The World Meteorological Organisation said there is an 80 per cent chance that an El Nino event develops between June and August.

title: Kremlin Accuses Ukraine Of Committing "Acts Of Terror" Against Civilians
content: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov made the remarks to reporters when asked about Russia's massive overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people and wounded 100.

title: 'Blacklist' Vendor Clause From CBSE Tender Removed In 2025, Documents Show
content: The CBSE said that even after the corrigendum, it still has the authority to blacklist a vendor anytime if serious violations are found

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Officer Says Renewed War With US 'Inevitable' Amid Trump's Peace Push
content: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal

title: 'Singam' K Annamalai: BJP's Tamil Nadu Star Rose Fast, Fell Faster
content: K Annamalai flew down to Delhi, met party chief Nitin Nabin on Tuesday and said he wants to end the partnership on "cordial terms".

title: "Monsoon Has Never Failed India In 13 Million Years": IIT Professor
content: Continuity does not mean stability. Paleoclimate records reveal that the monsoon has gone through dramatic fluctuations in the past. One of the most significant events occurred around 4,200 years ago, when a major drought hit large parts of Asia.

title: Bear Attack Leaves 4 Injured In North Japan
content: The bear was first spotted in a car parts factory, prompting an emergency call explaining that "employees had been bitten".

title: SKY's poor form continues; India T20 skipper out for 19 off 11 balls
content: Suryakumar Yadav was out for 19 off 11 balls as his team lost by 21 runs against north Mumbai Panthers at the Wankhede Stadium . the 35-year-old captaining triumph Knights in their opening match of season 4 of the T20 Mumbai League was restricted to 188 .
